verdict: AI displacement risk is extremely high due to rapid advancements in deep learning.

reasoning_steps.title: Rapid progress in deep learning
reasoning_steps.body: Advances in deep learning have allowed neural networks to surpass many previous machine learning approaches in performance, making the AI capability gap extremely large.

reasoning_steps.title: Highly routine cognitive tasks
reasoning_steps.body: Tasks such as analyzing and processing large sets of data using statistical software are highly routine and can be easily automated by current frontier LLMs.

evidence_ledger.contribution: Confirmed the high routine cognitive nature of ML tasks

evidence_ledger.meta: Exposure scores for AI in various occupations
evidence_ledger.contribution: Confirmed the extremely large AI capability gap for ML Engineers

adjacent_careers.name: Data Scientist
adjacent_careers.reasoning: ML Engineers and Data Scientists often work together, but Data Scientists have more creative judgment and regulatory moats

adjacent_careers.name: Software Engineer
adjacent_careers.reasoning: ML Engineers use software engineering skills to develop and implement machine learning models, but Software Engineers focus on broader software development tasks

recent_developments.headline: Google Announces Breakthrough in Deep Learning
recent_developments.impact: This development further emphasizes the rapid progress in deep learning and its potential to displace ML Engineers.

recent_developments.headline: AI coding assistants see broad enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Major firms report 25–30% of new code is now AI-generated and reviewed by engineers.

quotes.quote: Software engineering will be very different by the end of 2025. Most code will be written by AI, with human engineers reviewing and directing.
quotes.speaker: Dario Amodei
quotes.title: CEO, Anthropic

quotes.quote: AI is going to be the biggest technological shift of our lifetimes. Bigger than mobile, bigger than the web. Inside Microsoft, more than 30 percent of the code in some projects is now written by AI.
quotes.speaker: Satya Nadella
quotes.title: CEO, Microsoft

quotes.quote: More than a quarter of new code at Google is generated by AI, then reviewed and accepted by engineers.
quotes.speaker: Sundar Pichai
quotes.title: CEO, Google / Alphabet
